page: UpCloud PREMIUM-48xCPU-256GB is a general-purpose virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 9575F processor with 48 physical cores and 48 vCPUs, operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing approximately 5.33 GB of memory per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 500 GB SSD, and the configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. The CPU architecture is x86_64 and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, cryptography extensions such as AES, VAES, and SHA_NI, and virtualization support via a hypervisor flag.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core scoring 3631.57. Multi-core performance is also strong, scoring 91465.13 in stress-ng. Memory performance shows a distinct tradeoff: small-block cached read-only memory bandwidth is highly efficient at 5450.04 GB/sec, whereas large-block uncached read and write bandwidths drop to average levels of 62.46 GB/sec and 63.91 GB/sec respectively. For LLM workloads, the server achieves top-tier prompt processing speeds of 3689.76 tokens/sec on small 135M models, but drops to a weak 17.86 tokens/sec for medium 7B models, indicating a limitation for larger model inference without dedicated GPU acceleration.

Given its high core count and balanced memory allocation, this server is well-suited for multi-threaded CPU-bound workloads, database operations, and static web serving. Passmark database operations scored 26036.14, and Redis SET operations reached 68.18 million operations per second, confirming its capability for high-throughput data store hosting. The qualitative cost efficiency of this instance is characterized by its high physical core density and bundled local SSD storage, which reduces the need for external block storage attachments for standard database and web serving deployments.
description: The UpCloud PREMIUM-48xCPU-256GB is a high-capacity general-purpose server powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor with 48 physical cores and 48 vCPUs. Running on the KVM hypervisor, it features 256.0 GB of RAM (5.33 GB per core) and a bundled 500 GB local SSD. Performance benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU execution and strong multi-core capabilities, alongside high-throughput Redis SET operations at 68.18 million operations per second. However, memory bandwidth drops to average levels for large-block uncached operations, and LLM prompt processing is weak for medium-sized models. This server is highly efficient for multi-threaded applications, database hosting, and static web serving, offering a dense resource profile without requiring external storage add-ons.
og_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-48xCPU-256GB is a KVM-based server with 48 AMD EPYC cores, 256 GB RAM, and a 500 GB SSD, delivering strong multi-core performance and high database throughput.
meta_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-48xCPU-256GB features 48 AMD EPYC cores, 256 GB RAM, and 500 GB SSD storage, optimized for databases and multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A high-core x86_64 virtual server featuring AMD EPYC processing, balanced memory, and local SSD storage for demanding multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor with 48 physical cores and 48 vCPUs
Equipped with 256 GB of RAM and a bundled 500 GB local SSD
Top-tier single-core performance and strong multi-core benchmark results
Delivers high database throughput with 68.18 million Redis SET operations per second
Best suited for multi-threaded applications, database hosting, and high-volume web serving
